2.1: These are the sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun,
2.2: Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
2.3: The sons of Judah: Her, Onan, and Shelah, these three were born to him by the daughter of Shuh the Canaanite. Her, Judah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the Lord, who put him to death.
2.4: Tamar, Judah's daughter-in-law, bore him Perez and Zerah. All the sons of Judah were five in number.
2.5: Sons of Peres: Hesron and Hamul.
2.6: Sons of Zara: Zamri ethan, Eman, Chalchal and Dara: five in all.
2.7: Son of Charmi: Achar, who troubled Israel by transgressing concerning a thing devoted to anathema.
2.9: Sons who were born in Hesron: Jerahmeel, Ram and Calubi.
2.10: Ram begat Aminadab, Aminadab begat Nahasson, prince of the children of Judah,
2.11: Nahasson begot Salma, Salma begat Boaz,
2.12: Boaz begat Obed, Obed begat Jesse,
2.13: Jesse fathered Eliab his firstborn, Abinadab the second, and Simmaah the third.,
2.14: Nathanael, the fourth, Raddai, the fifth,
2.15: Asom, the sixth, and David, the seventh.
2.16: Their sisters were: Sarvia and Abigail. Sons of Sarvia: Abisai, Joab and Asahel, three.
2.17: Abigail gave birth to Amasa, whose father was Jether the Ishmaelite.
2.18: Caleb, son of Hezron, had children by Azubah, his wife, and Jerioth. These were the sons he had by Azubah: Jazer, Zobab, and Ardon.
2.19: Azuba died and Caleb took Ephrathah as his wife, who bore him Hur.
2.20: Hur begat Uri and Uri begat Bezalel.
2.21: Then Hezron married the daughter of Machir, father of Gilead: he was sixty years old when he married her, and she bore him Segub.
2.22: Segub fathered Jair, who had twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead.
2.23: The Gessurians and the Syrians took from them the towns of Jair, as well as Canath and its dependencies: sixty towns. All these were sons of Machir, father of Gilead.
2.24: After Hesron's death at Caleb-Ephrata, Abijah, Hesron's wife, bore him Ashur, the father of Tekua.
2.25: The sons of Jerahmeel, Hezron’s firstborn, were: Ram the firstborn, Bunah, Aran, Asom, and Ahijah.
2.26: Jerahmeel had another wife, named Athara, who was the mother of Onam.
2.27: The sons of Ram, the firstborn of Jerahmeel, were: Moash, Jamin, and Achar.
2.28: The sons of Onam were: Shemei and Jada. The sons of Shemei: Nadab and Abisur.
2.29: The name of Abisur's wife was Abihail and she bore him Ahobban and Molid.
2.30: Nadab's sons: Saled and Apphaim. Saled died without sons.
2.31: Son of Apphaim: Jesi. Son of Jesi: Shesan. Son of Shesan: Ohalai.
2.32: The sons of Jada, brother of Shemei: Jether and Jonathan. Jether died without sons.
2.33: Jonathan's sons: Phaleth and Ziza. These are the sons of Jerahmeel.
2.34: Seshan had no sons, but he had daughters. He had an Egyptian slave named Jera,
2.35: He gave him his daughter as a wife, and she bore him Athena.
2.36: Ethei begat Nathan, Nathan begat Zabad,
2.37: Zabad begat Ophlal, Ophlal begat Obed,
2.38: Obed begat Jehu, Jehu begat Azariah,
2.39: Azarias begat Helles, Helles begat Elasa,
2.40: Elasa begat Sisamoi, Sisamoi begat Sellum,
2.41: Sellum begat Icamias, and Icamias begat Elisama.
2.42: The sons of Caleb, brother of Jerahmeel: Mesha, his firstborn, who was the father of Ziph and the sons of Mareshah, father of Hebron.
2.43: Sons of Hebron: Korah, Thaphua, Recem and Samma.
2.44: Samma begat Raham, father of Jercaam, Récem begat Sammaï.
2.45: Son of Sammai: Maon and Maon was father of Bethsurus.
2.46: Ephah, Caleb's concubine, bore Haran, Moses, and Gezzet. Haran fathered Gezzet.
2.47: Sons of Jahaddai: Regom, Joathan, Gosan, Phalet, Ephah and Saaph.
2.48: Maacha, Caleb's concubine, bore Saber and Tharana.
2.49: She also bore Saaph, father of Madmenah, and Shuh, father of Mahbena and father of Gabaah. Caleb's daughter was Ahsah.
2.50: These were the sons of Caleb. Sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah: Zobal, father of Cariathariah,
2.51: Salma, father of Bethlehem, Hariph, father of Bethgader.
2.52: The sons of Sobal, father of Cariathariah, were: Haroe, Hatsi-Hammenuhoth.
2.53: The families of Cariatharia were: the Jethreans, the Aphuthites, the Shematheans, and the Maserites. From them came the Zareans and the Eshtaolites.
2.54: Salma's sons: Bethlehem and the Netophathites, Ataroth-Beth-Joab, half of the Manachites, the Zareites,
2.55: as well as the families of the Scribes residing in Jabesh, namely, the Tirites, the Shimatites, and the Sucathites. These are the Kinites, descended from Hamath, the father of the house of Rechab.

3.1: These are the sons of David who were born to him in Hebron: the firstborn, Amnon, son of Achinoam of Jezreel; the second, Daniel, son of Abigail of Carmel.
3.2: The third, Absalom, son of Maah, daughter of Tolmai, king of Geshur. The fourth, Adonijah, son of Haggith.
3.3: The fifth, Saphatias of Abital. The sixth, Jethraham of Egla his wife.
3.4: These six were born to him in Hebron, where he reigned for seven years and six months, then he reigned for thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
3.5: And these were born to him in Jerusalem: Simmaah, Zobab, Nathan, Solomon, four, by Bethshua, daughter of Ammiel,
3.9: These are all the sons of David, not counting the sons of the concubines. Tamar was their sister.
3.10: Solomon's sons: Rehoboam, Abijah his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son,
3.11: Joram his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son,
3.12: Amazias his son, Azarias his son, Joatham his son,
3.13: Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son,
3.14: Amon his son, Josiah his son.
3.15: The sons of Josiah: the firstborn Johanan, the second Joakim, the third Zedekiah, the fourth Shellum.
3.16: Sons of Joakim: Jeconiah his son, Zedekiah his son.
3.17: The sons of Jeconiah: Asir, and Shealtiel his son,
3.18: Melchiram, Phadaia, Senneser, Jecemias, Sama and Nadabias.
3.19: Sons of Phadaias: Zerubbabel and Semei. Sons of Zerubbabel: Mosollam, Hananiah and Salomith, their sister
3.20: and Hasaban, Ohol, Barachias, Hasadias, Josabhesed: five.
3.21: Sons of Hananiah: Phaltiah and Jezeiah, the sons of Raphaiah, the sons of Arnan, the sons of Obdiah, the sons of Shecheniah.
3.22: Son of Sechenias: Semeia. Sons of Semeia: Hattus, Jegaal, Baria, Naaria, Saphat: six.
3.23: The sons of Naariah: Elioenai, Hezekiah and Ezricam: three.
3.24: Sons of Elioenai: Oduiah, Eliasub, Pheleiah, Accub, Johanan, Dalaiah and Anani: seven.

4.1: Sons of Judah: Phares, Hezron, Charmi, Hur and Sobal.
4.2: Raah son of Sobal, fathered Jahath, Jahath fathered Ahamai and Lahad. These are the families of the Zareites.
4.3: These are the descendants of Etam's father: Jezreel, Jesema, and Jedebos; the name of their sister was Asalelphuni.
4.4: Phanuel was the father of Gedor and Ezer the father of Hosha. These were the sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah, the father of Bethlehem.
4.5: Assur, father of Thécué, had two wives: Halaa and Naara.
4.6: Naara bore him Oozam, Hepher, Temani and Ahasthari: these are the sons of Naara.
4.7: Sons of Halaa: Sereth, Isaar and Ethnan.
4.8: Cos fathered Anob and Sobobah and the families of Aharehel, son of Arum.
4.9: Jabez was more honored than his brothers; his mother named him Jabez, saying, "It is because I bore him in pain."«
4.10: Jabez called upon the God of Israel, saying, «If you will bless me and enlarge my territory, if your hand will be with me and keep me from harm, so that I may not be afflicted.» And God granted him what he had asked.
4.11: Kélub, brother of Sua, fathered Mahir, who was the father of Esthon.
4.12: Esthon fathered the house of Rapha, Phesse, and Tehinna, the father of the city of Naas. These were the men of Rehah.
4.13: Sons of Cenez: Othniel and Saraiah. Sons of Othniel: Hathath and Maonathi,
4.14: Maonathi fathered Ophra, Saraiah fathered Joab, father of those who lived in the valley of Charashim, for they were workers.
4.15: The sons of Caleb, the son of Jephon: Hir, Elah, and Maham. Sons of’ E la: Cénez.
4.16: Sons of Jaléléel: Ziph, Zipha, Tiria and Asraël.
4.17: The sons of Ezra: Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. Mered's wife, the Egyptian, bore Miriam, Shammai, and Jezeba, the father of Eshtamo.
4.18: His other wife, the Jewish woman, bore Jared, father of Gedor; Heber, father of Socho; and Icutiel, father of Zanoé. These were the sons of Bethiah, daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered had taken as his wife.
4.19: Son of the wife of Odaiah, sister of Naham: the father of Ceilah the Garmian and the father of Eshme the Machathian.
4.20: The sons of Simon: Amnon, Rinna, Ben-Hanan and Tilon. The sons of Jesi: Zoheth and Ben-Zoheth.
4.21: The sons of Sela, the sons of Judah: Her, the father of Leha, Laada, the father of Mareshah, and the families of the house where the fine linen is worked, of the house of Ashbeah
4.22: and Jokim and the men of Cozeba and Joash and Saraph, who ruled over Moab and Jashub-lechem. These are ancient things.
4.23: These were the potters and the inhabitants of Netaim and Gedera; they lived there, near the king, working for him.
4.24: Simeon's sons: Namuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, Saul.
4.25: Sellum his son, Madsam his son, Masma his son.
4.26: The sons of Masma: Hamuel his son, Zachur his son, and Semei his son.
4.27: Shemei had sixteen sons and six daughters, his brothers did not have many sons and all their families did not multiply as much as the sons of Judah.
4.28: They lived in Bersabée, Molada, and Hasar-Suhal.
4.29: to Bala, to Asom, to Tholad
4.30: in Bathuel, in Horma, in Sicéleg
4.31: to Beth-marshabot, to Hasar-Susim, to Beth-berai, and to Saarim: these were their cities until the reign of David
4.32: and their villages. They also had: Etam, Aen, Remmon, Thochen, and Asan, five cities
4.33: and all their villages, which were in the vicinity of these cities, as far as Baal. These are their dwellings and their genealogical records.
4.34: Mosobab, Jemlech, Jose son of Amaziah,
4.35: Joel, Jehu son of Josabiah son of Saraiah son of Asiel,
4.36: Elioenai, Jacoba, Isuhaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Ismiel, Banaiah,
4.37: Ziza son of Sephei, son of Allon son of Idaiah son of Semri son of Samaiah.
4.38: These men, identified by name, were princes in their families. Their families having grown considerably,
4.39: They went towards the entrance of Gador, to the east of the valley, in order to seek pastures for their flocks.
4.40: They found rich and good pastures and a spacious, quiet and peaceful territory; those who previously lived there came from Ham.
4.41: These men, recorded by name, arrived in the time of Hezekiah, king of Judah, they destroyed their tents, as well as the Meunites who were there, and, having devoted them to anathema to this day, they settled in their place, for there were pastures there for their flocks.
4.42: Some of Simeon’s sons also went to Mount Seir, five hundred men in number, with Phaltiah, Naariah, Raphaiah, and Uziel, son of Jeshi, at their head.
4.43: They defeated the remaining Amalekites who had escaped and they settled there to this day.

5.1: Reuben's son, firstborn of Israel. For he was the firstborn, but because he lay with one of his father's wives, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph, son of Israel, not, however, so that Joseph would be recorded in the genealogies as the firstborn.
5.2: For Judas was powerful among his brothers, and from him came a prince. But the birthright belongs to Joseph.
5.3: The sons of Reuben, firstborn of Israel: Enoch, Phallu, Hezron, and Charmi.
5.4: Joel's sons: Samia his son, Gog his son, Shemei his son,
5.5: Michael his son, Reiah his son, Baal his son,
5.6: Beera his son, whom Thelgathphalnasar, king of Assyria, took captive: he was one of the princes of the Reubenites.
5.7: Brothers of Beera, each according to his family, as they are recorded in the genealogies according to their generations: the chief, Jehiel, Zechariah,
5.8: Bala, son of Azaz, son of Shemah, son of Joel. Bala lived in Aroer and as far as Nebo and Beel-meon,
5.9: In the east, they lived as far as the entrance to the desert, which goes from the river Euphrates, for their flocks were numerous in the land of Gilead.
5.10: In the time of Saul, they waged war against the Hagareans, who fell into their hands, and they lived in their tents throughout the eastern part of Gilead.
5.11: The sons of Gad lived opposite them in the land of Bashan, as far as Selcha.
5.12: They were: the chief, Joel, the second, Shaphan, then Janai and Shaphat in Bashan.
5.13: Their brothers, according to their families, were: Michael, Mosollam, Shebe, Jorai, Jachan, Zie and Heber: seven.
5.14: They were the sons of Abihail, son of Huri, son of Jara, son of Gilead, son of Michael, son of Jezehi, son of Jeddo, son of Buz.
5.15: Achi, son of Abdiel, son of Guni, was the head of their families.
5.16: They lived in Gilead, in Bashan and in the cities of their dependencies and in all the pastures of Sharon to their extremities.
5.17: All these sons of Gad were recorded in the genealogies of the time of Jotham, king of Judah, and of the time of Jeroboam, king of Israel.
5.18: The sons of Reuben, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had valiant men, bearing shield and sword, shooting the bow and trained for war, numbering forty-four thousand seven hundred and sixty, fit to go into the army.
5.19: They waged war against the Agareans, Jethur, Naphis, and Nodab.
5.20: They were helped against them, and the Hagareans and all who were with them were delivered into their hands. For they had cried out to God during the battle, and he had answered them, because they had trusted in him.
5.21: They seized their herds, fifty thousand camels, two hundred and fifty thousand sheep, two thousand donkeys, and one hundred thousand people.,
5.22: For many had fallen dead, because the war was from God. And they settled in their place until the captivity.
5.23: The sons of the half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land from Bashan to Baal-Hermon and to Sanir and to the mountain of Hermon; they were numerous.
5.24: These are the heads of their families: Epher, Jesi, Eliel, Ezriel, Jeremiah, Odoiah and Jediel, mighty men of valor, men of renown, heads of their families.
5.25: But they were unfaithful to the God of their fathers and they prostituted themselves by serving the gods of the peoples of the land whom God had destroyed before them.
5.26: The God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Phul, king of Assyria, and the spirit of Tehgath-phalanzar, king of Assyria, and Tehgath-phalanzar carried away captive the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, and he brought them to Halah, to Habor, to Ara, and to the river Gozan, where they have remained to this day.
5.27: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kaath, and Merari.
5.28: Sons of Caath: Amram, Isaar and Oziel.
5.29: The sons of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abiu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
5.30: Eleazar fathered Phinehas, Phinehas fathered Abisue,
5.31: Abisué begat Bocci, Bocci begat Ozi,
5.32: Ozi begat Zarayas, Zarayas begat Meraioth,
5.33: Meraioth begat Amarias, Amarias begat Ahitob,
5.34: Ahitob begat Zadoc, Zadoc begat Ahimaas,
5.35: Ahimaas begat Azarias, Azarias begat Johanan,
5.36: Johanan fathered Azariah: he was the one who served as priest in the house that Solomon built in Jerusalem,
5.37: Azarias begat Amarias, Amarias begat Ahitob,
5.38: Ahitob begat Sadoc, Sadoc begat Sellum,
5.39: Sellum begat Helcias, Helcias begat Azarias,
5.40: Azarias begat Saraïas, Saraïas begat Josedec.
5.41: Josedec went into exile when the Lord led Judah and Jerusalem into captivity through Nebuchadnezzar.

6.1: The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kaath, and Merari.
6.2: These are the names of Gersom's sons: Lobni and Semei.
6.3: Sons of Caath: Amram, Isaar, Hebron and Oziel.
6.4: The sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi. These are the families of Levi according to their fathers:
6.5: From Gersom: Lobni his son, Jahath his son, Zamma his son,
6.6: Joah his son, Addo his son, Zara his son, Jethrai his son.
6.7: Sons of Caath: Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Asir his son,
6.8: Elkanah his son, Abiasaph his son, Asir his son,
6.9: Thahath his son, Uriel his son, Ozias his son, Saul his son.
6.10: Sons of Elkanah: Amasai and Ahimoth,
6.11: Elkanah his son, Sophaid his son, Nahath his son,
6.12: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elkanah his son.
6.13: Samuel's sons: the firstborn Vasséni and Abijah.
6.14: Sons of Merari: Moholi, Lobni his son, Semei his son, Oza his son,
6.15: Sammaa his son, Haggia his son, Asaïa his son.
6.16: These are the ones David appointed to lead the singing in the house of the Lord, from the time the ark had a place of rest:
6.17: They served as singers before the Tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting until Solomon had built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem, and they served them according to their regulations.
6.18: These are the ones who officiated with their sons: From among the sons of the Kohathites: Heman the singer, son of Joel, son of Samuel,
6.19: son of Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Eliel, son of Tohu,
6.20: son of Suph, son of Elkanah, son of Mahath, son of Amasai,
6.21: son of Elkanah, son of Joel, son of Azariah, son of Zephaniah,
6.22: son of Tahath, son of Assir, son of Abiasaph, son of Korah,
6.23: son of Isaar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, son of Israel.
6.24: His brother Asaph, who stood at his right hand: Asaph, son of Barachiah, son of Shama,
6.25: son of Michael, son of Basaias, son of Melchiah,
6.26: son of Athanai, son of Zerah, son of Adaiah,
6.27: son of Ethan, son of Zammah, son of Shemei,
6.28: son of Jeth, son of Gersom, son of Levi.
6.29: The sons of Merari, their brothers, on the left: Ethan, son of Cusi, son of Abdi, son of Maloch,
6.30: son of Hasabias, son of Amaziah, son of Helkiah,
6.31: son of Amasai, son of Boni, son of Somer,
6.32: son of Moholi, son of Musi, son of Merari, son of Levi.
6.33: Their brothers, the Levites, were in charge of all the service of the tabernacle of the house of the Lord.
6.34: But Aaron and his sons burned the victims on the altar of burnt offerings and the incense on the altar of incense; they had to fulfill the whole ministry of the holy of holies and make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses, the servant of God, had commanded.
6.35: These are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abisue his son,
6.36: Bocci his son, Ozi his son, Zaraïas his son,
6.37: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitob his son,
6.38: Sadoc his son, Achimaas his son.
6.39: Here are their dwellings, according to the boundaries of their camps: To the sons of Aaron, of the family of the Caathites, chosen first by lot,
6.40: Hebron was given, in the land of Judah, along with the pastures surrounding it.,
6.41: but the territory of the city and its villages was given to Caleb, son of Jephone.
6.42: So the sons of Aaron were given the city of refuge Hebron, Lobnah, and its pastures,
6.43: Jether, Esthemo and his pastures, Helon and his pastures, Dabir and his pastures,
6.44: Asan and its pastures, Bethsemesh and its pastures,
6.45: From the tribe of Benjamin, Gabea and its pastures, Almath and its pastures, Anathoth and its pastures. Total of their cities: thirteen cities, according to their families.
6.46: The other sons of Caath received by lot ten cities from the families of the tribe, from the half-tribe of Manasseh.
6.47: The sons of Gersom, according to their families, had thirteen cities of the tribe of Issachar, of the tribe of Asher, of the tribe of Naphtali and of the tribe of Manasseh, in Bashan.
6.48: The sons of Merari, according to their families, received by lot twelve cities from the tribe of Reuben, the tribe of Gad, and the tribe of Zebulun.
6.49: The children of Israel gave the cities and their pastures to the Levites.
6.50: They assigned by lot, from the tribe of the children of Judah, from the tribe of the children of Simeon, and from the tribe of the children of Benjamin, these cities which they designated by their names.
6.51: For the other families of the sons of Kaath, the cities that fell to them were of the tribe of Ephraim.
6.52: They were given: the city of refuge Shechem and its pastures in the hill country of Ephraim, Gazer and its pastures,
6.53: Jecmaam and his pastures, Bethoron and his pastures,
6.54: Helon and its pastures, Geth-Remmon and its pastures
6.55: and of the half-tribe of Manasseh, Aner and his pastures, Balaam and his pastures: for the families of the other sons of Kohath.
6.56: To the sons of Gersom, of the family of the half-tribe of Manasseh, were given Gaulon in Bashan and its pastures, Ashtaroth and its pastures,
6.57: of the tribe of Issachar, Kedesh and its pastures, Dabereth and its pastures,
6.58: Ramoth and its pastures, Anem and its pastures,
6.59: of the tribe of Asher, Masal and its pastures, Abdon and its pastures,
6.60: Hucac and its pastures, Rohob and its pastures,
6.61: and of the tribe of Naphtali, Cedes in Galilee and its pastures, Hamon and its pastures, and Caryathaim and its pastures.
6.62: To the remaining Levites, the sons of Merari, of the tribe of Zebulun, were given Remmon and its pastures, Tabor and its pastures,
6.63: on the other side of the Jordan, opposite Jericho, east of the Jordan: from the tribe of Reuben, Beor, to the desert and its pastures, Jassa and its pastures,
6.64: Kademot and its pastures, Mephaat and its pastures,
6.65: and of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth-gilead and its pastures, Manaim and its pastures,
6.66: Hesebon and its pastures, Jezer and its pastures.

7.1: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Jasub and Simeron: four.
7.2: Sons of Tola: Uzi, Raphaiah, Jeriel, Jemai, Jebsem and Samuel, heads of their families from Tola, mighty warriors, listed according to their generations, in the days of David, numbering twenty-two thousand six hundred.
7.3: Sons of Uzi: Jezreah. Sons of Jezreah: Michael, Obadiah, Johel and Jesiah: five leaders in all.
7.4: They had with them, according to their generations, according to their families, armed troops for war, thirty-six thousand men, because they had many wives and sons.
7.5: Their brothers, according to all the families of Issachar, valiant warriors, numbered a total of eighty-seven thousand, recorded in the genealogies.
7.6: Benjamin's sons: Béla, Béchor and Jadiel: three.
7.7: Sons of Bela: Eshbon, Uzi, Uziel, Jerome and Urai: five heads of families, valiant warriors, recorded in the genealogies, numbering twenty-two thousand four hundred.
7.8: The sons of Behor: Zamirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Amri, Jerimot, Abijah, Anathoth, and Almath: all these sons of Behor,
7.9: recorded in the genealogies, according to their generations, as heads of their families, valiant warriors, numbering twenty thousand two hundred.
7.10: Balan, son of Jadihel. Jehus, Benjamin, Ehud, Canaan, Zethan, Tarshish, and Abisahar:
7.11: all these sons of Jadihel, heads of their families, valiant warriors, numbering seventeen thousand two hundred, ready to go to war with arms.
7.12: Sepham and Hapham, sons of Hir: Hasim, son of Aher.
7.13: The sons of Naphtali: Jasiel, Guni, Jeser and Shellum, sons of Bala.
7.14: Son of Manasseh: Esriel. His Syrian concubine bore Machir, father of Gilead.
7.15: Machir took a wife from Happhim and Saphan; his sister's name was Maacha. The name of the second son was Salphaad, and Salphaad had daughters.
7.16: Maacha, wife of Machir, gave birth to a son and named him Peres, the name of his brother was Sares and his sons were Ulam and Recen.
7.17: The sons of Ulam: Badan. These are the sons of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh.
7.18: His sister Hammoleket gave birth to Ishmael, Abiezer, and Moholah.
7.19: The sons of Shemidah were: Ahin, Shechem, Lehi, and Aniam.
7.20: The sons of Ephraim: Suthalah, Bared his son, Tahath his son, Elada his son, Tahath his son,
7.21: Zabad his son, Suthala his son, Ezer and Elad, whom the men of Geth born in the land killed, because they had come down to take their flocks.
7.22: Ephraim, their father, mourned for a long time, and his brothers came to comfort him.
7.23: And he went to his wife and she conceived and gave birth to a son, and he named him Beriah, because there was trouble in his house.
7.24: His daughter was Sarah, who built Lower Bethoron and Upper Bethoron and Ozen-Sarah.
7.25: Rapha his son and Reseph, Thale his son, Taan his son,
7.26: Laadan his son, Ammiud his son, Elisama his son,
7.27: Nun his son, Joshua his son.
7.28: Their possessions and dwellings were: Bethel and its dependencies on the east, Noran on the west, Gezer and its dependencies, Shechem and its dependencies, as far as Aza and its dependencies.
7.29: In the hands of the sons of Manasseh were still Bethsan and its dependent towns, Thanah and its dependent towns, Mageddo and its dependent towns, Dor and its dependent towns. It was in these towns that the sons of Joseph, the sons of Israel, lived.
7.30: Sons of Asher: Jemmah, Jeshua, Jessui, Bariah, and Sarah, their sister.
7.31: Sons of Bariah: Heber and Melchiel, who was the father of Barsaith.
7.32: Heber fathered Jephthah, Somer, Hotham, and Suaa, their sister.
7.33: The sons of Jephthah: Phoseh, Shamaal and Ashoth: these are the sons of Jephthah.
7.34: Sons of Somer: Ahi, Roaga, Haba and Aram.
7.35: Son of Helem, his brother: Supha, Jemna, Selles and Amal.
7.36: Sons of Supha: Sue, Harnapher, Sual, Beri, Jamra,
7.37: Bosor, Hod, Samma, Salusa, Jethran and Bera.
7.38: Sons of Jether: Jephon, Phaspha, and Ara.
7.39: Olla's sons: Arée, Haniel and Résia.
7.40: All these were sons of Asher, heads of families, elite and valiant men, leaders of princes, registered as twenty-six thousand men fit to go to war under arms.

8.1: Benjamin fathered Béla, his firstborn, Asbel the second, and Ahara the third.,
8.2: Nohaa, the fourth, and Rapha, the fifth.
8.3: The sons of Béla were: Addar, Gera, Abiud,
8.6: These are the sons of Ahod: they were the heads of the families who lived in Gibeah, and they deported them to Manahath:
8.7: Naaman, Achia and Gera, he was the one who deported them and he fathered Oza and Ahiud.
8.8: Saharaim had children in the land of Moab, after he had sent away his wives Husim and Bara.
8.9: He had by Hodesh, his wife: Jobab, Sebiah, Moses, Molshom,
8.10: Jehus, Shechia and Marmah, these are his sons, heads of families.
8.11: He had by Husim: Abitob and Elphaal.
8.12: Sons of Elphaal: Heber, Misaam and Samad, who built Ono, Lod and the cities under its jurisdiction.
8.13: Baria and Sama, heads of the families who lived in Aïalon, put the inhabitants of Geth to flight.
8.16: Michael, Jespha, and Joha were sons of Bariah.
8.18: Jesamari, Jezliah and Jobab were sons of Elphaal.
8.21: Adaiah, Baraiah and Samarath were sons of Shemei.
8.25: Jephdaiah and Phanuel were sons of Shesach.
8.27: Jersiah, Elijah and Zechariah were the sons of Jeroham.
8.28: These were heads of families, heads according to their generations, they lived in Jerusalem.
8.29: Gibeon's father lived in Gibeon and his wife's name was Maacha.
8.30: His firstborn son, Abdon, then Sur, Cis, Baal, Nadab,
8.32: Macelloth fathered Samaah. They also lived near their brothers in Jerusalem, with their brothers.
8.33: Ner begat Cis, Cis begat Saul, Saul begat Jonathan, Melchisua, Abinadab and Esbaal.
8.34: Son of Jonathan: Meribbaal, Meribbaal begot Micha.
8.35: Micha's sons: Phiton, Melech, Tharaa and Ahaz.
8.36: Ahaz begat Joada, Joada begat Alamath, Azmoth and Zamri, Zamri begat Mosa.
8.37: Mosa fathered Banaa, Rapha his son, Elasa his son, Asel his son.
8.38: Asel had six sons, whose names were: Ezricam, Bocru, Ishmael, Saria, Obdiah and Hanan: all these were sons of Asel.
8.39: Esech's son, his brother: Ulam, his firstborn, Jehus the second, and Eliphalet the third.
8.40: The sons of Ulam were valiant men, skilled with the bow, and they had many sons and grandsons, one hundred and fifty. All these are sons of Benjamin.

9.1: Thus all Israel was recorded in the genealogies, and behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Israel, and Judah was taken captive to Babylon because of its transgressions.
9.2: The first inhabitants, who remained in their possessions, in their cities, were the Israelites, the priests, the Levites, and the Nathinaeans.
9.3: In Jerusalem lived descendants of Judah, descendants of Benjamin, descendants of Ephraim, and descendants of Manasseh.,
9.4: sons of Peresh, sons of Judah: Othai, son of Ammiud, son of Amri, son of Omrai, son of Bonni,
9.5: of the Silonites: Asaiah, the firstborn, and his sons,
9.6: of the sons of Zerah: Jehuel and their brothers: six hundred and ninety,
9.7: The sons of Benjamin: Salo, son of Mosollam, son of Oduiah, son of Asana,
9.8: Jobaniah, son of Jeroham; Elah, son of Uzi, son of Mochori; Mosollam, son of Shaphatiah, son of Rahuel, son of Jebaniah;,
9.9: and their brothers, according to their generations: nine hundred and fifty-six. All these men were heads of families according to their families.
9.11: Azariah, son of Helkiah, son of Mosollam, son of Zadok, son of Maraioth, son of Ahitob, prince of the house of God,
9.12: Adaiah, son of Jeroham, son of Phashr, son of Melkiah, Maasai, son of Adiel, son of Jezra, son of Mosollam, son of Mosollamith, son of Emmer,
9.13: and their brothers, heads of families: one thousand seven hundred and sixty valiant warriors, for the work of serving the house of God.
9.14: Levites: Shemeiah, son of Hassub, son of Ezricam, son of Hasebiah, of the sons of Merari,
9.15: Bacbachar, Heres, Galal, Mattaniah, son of Micah, son of Zechariah, son of Asaph,
9.16: Obdia, son of Shemeiah, son of Galal, son of Idithun, Barachiah, son of Asa, son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
9.17: And the gatekeepers: Sellum, Accub, Telmon, Ahiman and their brothers, Sellum was the leader.,
9.18: and it is still at the king's gate, to the east. These are the gatekeepers for the camp of the children of Levi.
9.19: Shallum, son of Korah, son of Abiasaph, son of Korah, and his brothers of his father's house, the Korahites, served as gatekeepers of the tabernacle; their fathers had guarded the entrance to the camp of the Lord.,
9.20: Phinehas, son of Eleazar, had once been their leader, and the Lord was with him.
9.21: Zacharie, son of Mosollamia, was the gatekeeper at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
9.22: All these men, chosen as gatekeepers, numbered two hundred and twelve, recorded in the genealogies of their villages; David and Samuel the seer had established them in their functions.
9.23: They and their children were at the gates of the house of the Lord, the house of the tabernacle, for the guards.
9.24: The gatekeepers stood at the four winds, to the east, to the west, to the north and to the south.
9.25: Their brothers, who remained in their villages, had to come to them from time to time, for a week.
9.26: For these four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were constantly on duty and they also had the supervision of the chambers and the treasuries in the house of God.
9.27: They lodged around the house of God, which they guarded, and they had to provide for its opening every morning.
9.28: Some of the Levites were in charge of the utensils used in the service, which they brought in and took out, counting them.
9.29: Others were in charge of the utensils, all the utensils of the sanctuary, and the fine flour, the wine, the oil, the incense, and the spices.
9.30: The sons of priests composed the aromatic perfumes.
9.31: One of the Levites, Mattathias, the firstborn son of the Sellum of Korahite, was in charge of the cakes baked on the pan.
9.32: And some of their brothers from among the sons of the Kaathites were charged with preparing the showbread for each Sabbath.
9.33: These were the singers, heads of Levite families, who lived in the chambers and were exempt from other duties, because they were at their work day and night.
9.34: These were the heads of Levite families, heads according to their generations; they lived in Jerusalem.
9.35: The father of Gibeon, Jehiel, lived in Gibeon and the name of his wife was Maah.
9.36: Abdon his firstborn son, then Sur, Cis, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
9.38: Macelloth fathered Samaan. They too lived near their brothers in Jerusalem, with their brothers.
9.39: Ner begat Cis, Cis begat Saul, Saul begat Jonathan, Melchisua, Abinadab and Esbaal.
9.40: Son of Jonathan: Meribbaal. Meribbaal begat Micha.
9.41: Sons of Micha: Phithon, Melech, Tharaa.
9.42: Ahaz begat Jara, Jara begat Alamath, Azmoth and Zamri, Zamri begat Mosa.
9.43: Mosa fathered Banaa. Raphaea his son, Elasa his son, Asel his son.
9.44: Asel had six sons, whose names are: Ezricam, Bocru, Ishmael, Sariah, Obdiah, and Hanan. These are the sons of Asel.

10.1: When the Philistines gave battle to Israel, the men of Israel fled before the Philistines and fell mortally wounded on Mount Gilboa.
10.2: The Philistines pursued Saul and his sons, and the Philistines killed Jonathan, Abinadab, and Melchisua, Saul's sons.
10.3: The fighting effort focused on Saul; the archers having discovered him, he was greatly afraid of the archers.
10.4: Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, «Draw your sword and run me through with it, lest these uncircumcised men come and insult me.» But the armor-bearer refused, for he was terrified; so Saul took his own sword and fell upon it.
10.5: When the armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he too fell upon his sword and died.
10.6: Thus perished Saul and his three sons, and his entire household perished at the same time.
10.7: All the men of Israel who were in the plain, seeing that the children of Israel had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, abandoned their cities and also fled, and the Philistines came and settled there.
10.8: The next day, the Philistines came to plunder the dead and they found Saul and his sons lying on Mount Gilboa.
10.9: Having stripped him, they took his head and his weapons, and then they sent to proclaim this good news throughout the land of the Philistines, before their idols and the people.
10.10: They put Saul's weapons in the house of their god and fastened his skull in the temple of Dagon.
10.11: When all of Jabez-Gilead heard of everything the Philistines had done to Saul,
10.12: All the worthy men arose, took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons and brought them to Jabesh. They buried their bones under the terebinth tree at Jabesh and fasted for seven days.
10.13: Saul died because of the transgression he had committed against the Lord, because he had not observed the word of the Lord and because he had inquired and consulted those who summon the dead.
10.14: He did not consult the Lord, and the Lord put him to death and transferred the kingship to David, son of Jesse.

11.1: All Israel gathered around David at Hebron, saying, «Behold, we are your bones and your flesh.
11.2: »Even in the past, when Saul was king, you were the one who led Israel back and forth. And the Lord your God said to you, ‘You will shepherd my people Israel, and you will be ruler over my people Israel.’”
11.3: So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a covenant with them before the Lord at Hebron, and they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the Lord spoken by Samuel.
11.4: David marched with all Israel against Jerusalem, which is Jebus, where the Jebusites, inhabitants of the land, were.
11.5: The inhabitants of Jebus said to David, "You shall not enter here." But David took possession of the stronghold of Zion: that is the city of David.
11.6: David said, "Whoever first defeats the Jebusites will be commander and prince." Joab, son of Zerviah, went up first and became commander.
11.7: David settled in the fortress, which is why it was called the City of David.
11.8: He built the city all around, starting from the Mello and in the surrounding area, and Joab repaired the rest of the city.
11.9: David grew greater and greater, and the Lord of hosts was with him.
11.10: These are the leaders of the heroes who served David and who, together with all Israel, helped him during his reign, after having helped to make him king, according to the word of the Lord concerning Israel.
11.11: This is the number of the heroes who served David: Jeshbaam, son of Hashamoni, leader of the Thirty. He wielded his spear against three hundred men, whom he killed at one time.
11.12: After him, Eleazar, son of Dodo the Ahohite, was among the three valiant ones.
11.13: He was with David at Phes-domim, and the Philistines had gathered there to fight. There was a piece of land there full of barley, and the people were fleeing before the Philistines.
11.14: They stood in the middle of the field, defended it, and defeated the Philistines, and the Lord brought about a great deliverance.
11.15: Three of the thirty captains went down to David on the rock at the cave of Odollam, while the Philistine camp was pitched in the valley of Rephaim.
11.16: David was then in the fortress and there was a Philistine outpost in Bethlehem.
11.17: David had a desire and said, «Who will get me water to drink from the cistern that is at the gate of Bethlehem?»
11.18: Immediately the three men, passing through the Philistine camp, drew water from the cistern at the gate of Bethlehem. They took it and brought it to David, but David refused to drink it and instead offered it as a libation to the Lord.,
11.19: saying, «God forbid that I should do this. Would I drink the blood of these men with their lives? For it was at the peril of their lives that they brought it.» And he refused to drink it. That is what these three brave men did.
11.20: Abishai, Joab’s brother, was the leader of the Three; he raised his spear against three hundred men and killed them, and he gained renown among the three.
11.21: He was the most highly regarded of the second triad and was their leader, but he did not equal the first three.
11.22: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, son of a valiant and wondrous man of Cabseel, struck down the two Ariels of Moab. He went down and struck down the lion in the middle of the cistern on a snowy day.
11.23: He struck an Egyptian who was five cubits tall, and in the Egyptian's hand was a spear like a loom roller. He went down to him with a staff, snatched the spear from the Egyptian's hand, and struck him with his own spear.
11.24: This is what Benaiah son of Jehoiada did, and he became renowned among the three mighty warriors.
11.25: He was now more highly regarded than the thirty, but he did not equal the three. David made him a member of his secret council.
11.26: Valiant warriors of the army: Asahel, brother of Joab, Elhanan, son of Dodo, of Bethlehem,
11.27: Sammoth of Arori, Helles of Phalon,
11.28: Hira son of Accès of Thécué, Abiéser of Anathot,
11.29: Sobbochai the Husathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
11.30: Maharai of Netopha, Heled son of Baana of Netopha,
11.31: Ethai son of Ribai of Gibeah, of the sons of Benjamin, Banaiah of Pharaoh,
11.32: Hurai of the valleys of Gaas, Abiel of Arabah, Azmaveth of Bauram, Eliaba of Shebon,
11.33: Bené-Assem of Gézon, Jonathan son of Sagé, of Arar,
11.34: Ahiam son of Sachar, from Arar,
11.36: Hepher of Meshera, Ahia of Pelon,
11.37: Hesro of Carmel, Naarai son of Asbai,
11.38: Joel, brother of Nathan, and Mibahar, son of Agarai,
11.39: Select the Ammonite, Naharai the Berothian, armor-bearer of Joab, son of Zeruiah,
11.40: Ira of Jether, Gareb of Jether,
11.41: Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Oholi,
11.42: Adina, son of Siza the Reubenite, leader of the Reubenites, and thirty others with him,
11.43: Hanan son of Maacha, Jehoshaphat of Mathan,
11.44: Ozia of Astaroth, Samma and Jehiel son of Hotham of Aroer,
11.45: Jedihel son of Samri, Joha his brother, the Tosaite,
11.46: Eliel of Mahum, Jeribai and Josaiah son of Elnaem, Jethmah the Moabite, [
11.47: Eliel, Obed and Jasiel, of Masobia.]

12.1: These are the ones who came to David at Shikleh, when he still had to stay away from the face of Saul, son of Cish; they were among the valiant ones who helped him in the war.
12.2: They were archers, skilled in throwing stones with their right hand and left hand and in shooting arrows with their bow; they were among the brothers of Saul, of Benjamin.
12.3: At their head were Ahiezer, then Joash, both sons of Shamah of Gibeah, Jaziel and Phallet, sons of Azmoth, Barachah, Jehu of Anathoth,
12.4: Samaiah of Gibeon, mighty among the Thirty and leader of the Thirty, Jeremiah, Jeheziel, Johanan, Jezabad of Gedera,
12.5: Eluzai, Jerimuth, Baaliah, Samariah, Saphatia, of Haruph,
12.6: Elkanah, Jesiah, Azareel, Joer, and Jeshbaam, sons of Korah,
12.7: Joella and Zabadiah, sons of Jeroham, of Gedor.
12.8: Among the Gadites, valiant warriors passed by to David in his refuge in the desert: warriors trained in battle, skilled in the use of shield and spear, having the appearance of lions, as agile as gazelles on the mountains.
12.9: Ezer was the chief, Obadiah the second, Eliab the third,
12.10: Masmana, the fourth, Jeremiah, the fifth,
12.11: Ethi, the sixth, Eliel, the seventh,
12.12: Johanan, the eighth, Elzébad, the ninth,
12.13: Jeremiah, the tenth, Machbanai, the eleventh.
12.14: They were sons of Gad, commanders of the army; one, the smallest, could prevail over a hundred, and the greatest over a thousand.
12.15: It was they who crossed the Jordan in the first month, when it overflows on all its banks, and who put to flight all the inhabitants of the valleys, to the east and to the west.
12.16: Some of the sons of Benjamin and Judah came to David in his refuge.
12.17: David went out to meet them, addressed them, and said: «If you have come to me with peaceful intentions, to help me, my heart will turn to you, to unite us; but if it is to betray me treacherously to my enemies, when no violence is attached to my hands, may the God of our fathers see it and avenge me.»
12.18: The Spirit came upon Amasai, the leader of the Thirty, and said, «To you, David, and with you, son of Jesse. Peace, peace to you and peace to him who helps you, for your God has helped you.» And David welcomed them and placed them among the leaders of the band.
12.19: Some men from Manasseh joined David when he went with the Philistines to fight against Saul. But they did not help the Philistines, because the Philistine leaders deliberately sent David away, saying, "At the risk of our lives, he would join his master Saul."«
12.20: As he returned to Zishaleg, some men from Manasseh joined him: Ednas, Jozabad, Jedihel, Michael, Jozabad, Eliu and Shelath, leaders of thousands of Manasseh.
12.21: They came to David's aid against the raiding parties, for they were all valiant men and leaders in the army.
12.22: And day by day men came to David to help him, until he had a great army, like an army of God.
12.23: This is the number of the commanders of the armed men for war who came to David at Hebron, to transfer to him the kingship of Saul, according to the command of the Lord.
12.24: Sons of Judah, bearing shield and spear, six thousand eight hundred, armed for war.
12.25: Seven thousand one hundred sons of Simeon, valiant warriors in the war.
12.26: Four thousand six hundred sons of Levi,
12.27: and Joiada, head of Aaron's family, and with him three thousand seven hundred
12.28: and Sadoc, a valiant young man, and his father's house, twenty-two chiefs.
12.29: Three thousand sons of Benjamin, brothers of Saul, for until then the greater part of them had remained loyal to the house of Saul.
12.30: Twenty thousand eight hundred sons of Ephraim, mighty warriors and renowned in their fathers' houses.
12.31: From the half-tribe of Manasseh, eighteen thousand, who were specifically designated to go and establish David as king.
12.32: The sons of Issachar, having understanding of the times to know what Israel should do, two hundred leaders and all their brothers under their command.
12.33: From Zebulun, fifty thousand, fit for service, ready for war and in perfect war equipment, to line up for battle with a resolute heart.
12.34: From Naphtali, a thousand leaders and with them thirty-seven thousand, bearing shield and spear.
12.35: Twenty-eight thousand six hundred Danites ready for war.
12.36: D'Aser, fit to go to the army and ready for war, forty thousand.
12.37: And on the other side of the Jordan, there were Reubenites, Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, fully equipped with weapons of war, one hundred and twenty thousand.
12.38: All these men of war, knowing how to arrange themselves in battle, came with a sincere heart to Hebron to establish David king over all Israel, and all the rest of Israel also had only one heart to establish David king.
12.39: They were there three days with David, eating and drinking, for their brothers had prepared provisions for them.
12.40: And even those who lived near them, as far as Issachar, Zebulun and Naphtali, brought provisions on donkeys, camels, mules and oxen, supplies of flour, masses of dried figs and raisins, wine, oil, oxen and sheep in abundance, for there was joy in Israel.

13.1: David held council with the leaders of thousands and hundreds, with all the princes.
13.2: And David said to all the congregation of Israel, «If it seems good to you, and if it appears to be from the Lord our God, let us send quickly to the rest of our brothers throughout all the regions of Israel, and also to the priests and Levites in the cities where their pastures are, so that they may join us,
13.3: and that we bring back to us the ark of our God, for we neglected it in the days of Saul.»
13.4: The whole assembly decided to do so, the matter having seemed appropriate in the eyes of all the people.
13.5: So David assembled all Israel, from the Sihor of Egypt to the entrance of Emath, to bring the ark of God from Cariathiarim.
13.6: And David, with all Israel, went up to Baalah, to Cariatharim, which is in Judah, to bring up from there the ark of God, of the Lord who sits upon the Cherubim, upon which is called the Name.
13.7: They placed the ark of God on a new cart and brought it from the house of Abinadab: Oza and Ahio drove the cart.
13.8: David and all Israel danced before God with all their might, singing and playing harps, lyres, tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
13.9: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Chidon, Oza stretched out his hand to grasp the ark, because the oxen had stumbled.
13.10: The Lord’s anger was kindled against Oza, and the Lord struck him down, because he had stretched out his hand on the ark, and Oza died there before God.
13.11: David was angry that the Lord had thus struck Oza, and that place has been called Pheretz-Oza to this day.
13.12: David was afraid of God that day and said, «How can I bring the ark of God to me?»
13.13: David did not bring the ark home to the city of David, but he had it taken to the house of Obededom of Geth.
13.14: The ark of God remained three months with the family of Obededom, in his house, and the Lord blessed the house of Obededom and all that belonged to him.

14.1: Hiram, king of Tyre, sent messengers to David, with cedar wood, as well as stonemasons and carpenters, to build him a house.
14.2: And David recognized that the Lord had established him as king over Israel, for his kingdom was exalted, because of his people Israel.
14.3: David took more wives in Jerusalem and fathered more sons and daughters.
14.4: These are the names of the children he had in Jerusalem: Samua, Sobad, Nathan, Solomon,
14.8: The Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, so all the Philistines went up to search for David. David heard of it and went out to meet them.
14.9: The Philistines, having arrived, rushed into the valley of Rephaim.
14.10: David inquired of God, saying, «Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will you deliver them into my hand?» And the Lord said to him, «Go up, and I will deliver them into your hand.»
14.11: They went up to Baal-Pharasim, and there David defeated them. And David said, «God has shattered my enemies by my hand, as waters shatter a dam.» Therefore the name of that place was given to Baal-Pharasim.
14.12: They left their gods there. And David said that they should be given over to the fire.
14.13: The Philistines rushed back into the valley.
14.14: David consulted God again and God said to him, «Do not go up after them, turn away from them and you will come upon them from the direction of the balsam trees.
14.15: When you hear the sound of footsteps in the tops of the balsam trees, then you will go out to fight, for God will go out before you to defeat the army of the Philistines.»
14.16: David did as God commanded him, and they defeated the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gazer.
14.17: David's fame spread throughout all countries, and the Lord made him feared by all nations.

15.1: He built houses for himself in the city of David and prepared a place for the ark of God and pitched a tent for it.
15.2: Then David said, «Only the Levites are to carry the ark of God, for they are the ones whom the Lord has chosen to carry the ark of God and to minister to it forever.»
15.3: David assembled all Israel at Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the Lord in its place, which he had prepared for it.
15.4: David gathered Aaron's sons and the Levites:
15.5: of the sons of Caath, Uriel the chief and his brothers: one hundred and twenty.
15.6: Of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief and his brothers: two hundred and twenty.
15.7: One hundred and thirty of Gersom's sons, Joel the chief and his brothers.
15.8: His sons from Elisaphan, Shemeiah the chief and his brothers: two hundred.
15.9: Eighty sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief and his brothers.
15.10: One hundred and twelve sons of Oziel, Aminadab the chief and his brothers.
15.11: David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar and the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemeiah, Eliel, and Amminadab
15.12: And he said to them, «You are the heads of the families of the Levites, consecrate yourselves, you and your brothers, and bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel, to the place I have prepared for it.
15.13: Because it was not you who struck us the first time, the Lord our God, because we had not sought him according to the law.»
15.14: The priests and Levites consecrated themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord, the God of Israel.
15.15: And the sons of Levi, as Moses had commanded according to the word of the Lord, carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles.
15.16: David told the leaders of the Levites to appoint their brothers the singers with musical instruments, lyres, harps and cymbals, to make bright and joyful sounds.
15.17: The Levites appointed Heman son of Joel and, among his brothers, Asaph son of Barachiah, among the sons of Merari, their brothers Ethan son of Cusaia,
15.18: and, with them, their brothers of the second order, Zechariah, Ben, Jaziel, Semiramoth, Jahiel, Ani, Eliab, Benaiahs, Maaziahs, Mattathias, Eliphalu, Maceniahs, Obededom and Jehiel, the gatekeepers.
15.19: The singers Heman, Asaph and Ethan had bronze cymbals to make them resound.
15.20: Zechariah, Oziel, Semiramoth, Jahiel, Ani, Eliab, Maasiah and Banai had lyres made of alamoth.
15.21: Mathathias, Eliphalu, Macenias, Obededom, Jehiel and Ozaziu had harps at the lower octave, to preside over the singing.
15.22: Chonénias, chief of the Levites for transport, directed the transport because he knew how to do it.
15.23: Barachias and Elcana were gatekeepers at the ark.
15.24: Shebeniah, Jehoshaphat, Nathanael, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, blew the trumpets before the ark of God. Obededom and Jehiah were gatekeepers beside the ark.
15.25: David, the elders of Israel, and the leaders of thousands set out to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from the House of Obededom, amid joy.
15.26: When God had assisted the Levites who carried the ark of the covenant of the Lord, six bulls and six rams were sacrificed.
15.27: David was covered with a byssus cloak, as were all the Levites who carried the ark, the singers, and Chonenias, who directed the transport of the ark, among the singers, and David had on him a linen ephod.
15.28: All Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the Lord with shouts of joy, with the sound of trumpets, bugles and cymbals, and with the playing of lyres and harps.
15.29: When the ark of the covenant of the Lord arrived at the city of David, Michal, daughter of Saul, looked out of the window and saw King David leaping and dancing, she despised him in her heart.

16.1: After the ark of God had been brought in and placed in the middle of the tent that David had pitched for it, burnt offerings and peace offerings were offered before God.
16.2: When David had finished offering the burnt offerings and the peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the Lord.
16.3: Then he distributed to all the Israelites, both men and women, to each a loaf of bread, a portion of meat, and a cake of raisins.
16.4: He appointed Levites before the ark of the Lord to minister, invoking, celebrating, and praising the Lord, the God of Israel.
16.5: These were: Asaph, the chief, Zechariah, the second, then Jahiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattathias, Eliab, Benaiahs, Obededom and Jehiel, with lyres and harps as instruments, and Asaph sounded the cymbals.
16.6: The priests Banaiah and Jaziel continually blew their trumpets before the ark of God's covenant.
16.7: It was on this day that David first established that the Lord would be praised through Asaph and his brothers:
16.8: Praise the Lord, call upon his name, make known his works among the peoples.
16.9: Sing in his honor, sing psalms in his honor, tell of all his wonders.
16.10: Glory in his holy name, let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
16.11: Seek the Lord and his strength; seek his face continually.
16.12: Remember the wonders he performed, his miracles, and the judgments he pronounced.,
16.13: The race of Israel, his servant; the children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
16.14: He, the Lord, is our God; his judgments are exercised throughout the earth.
16.15: Remember his covenant forever. The word he spoke for a thousand generations.
16.16: From the covenant he made with Abraham and his oath to Isaac,
16.17: which he established as a law for Jacob, as an everlasting covenant for Israel,
16.18: saying, "To you I will give the land of Canaan as your inheritance."«
16.19: While you were easy to count, few in number, and foreigners in the country,
16.20: that they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another people,
16.21: He allowed no one to oppress them, and he punished kings on their account.,
16.22: «Do not touch my anointed ones, and do not harm my prophets.»
16.23: Sing to the Lord, all the earth; proclaim his salvation day after day.
16.24: Declare his glory among the nations, his wonders among all peoples.
16.25: For the Lord is great and most worthy of praise; he is to be feared above all gods.
16.26: for all the gods of the peoples are idols. And the Lord made the heavens,
16.27: Majesty and splendor are before his face; strength and joy are in his dwelling place.
16.28: Ascribe to the Lord, families of the peoples, ascribe to the Lord glory and power.,
16.29: Give to the Lord glory for his name. Bring offerings and come before him, worship the Lord in holy attire,
16.30: Tremble before him, inhabitants of all the earth, and the established world will not be shaken.
16.31: Let the heavens rejoice and let the earth be glad. Let it be said among the nations, «The Lord is king.»
16.32: Let the sea resound with all that it contains. Let the fields tremble with all that they contain.
16.33: Let the trees of the forest shout for joy before the Lord, for he comes to judge the earth.
16.34: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his mercy endures forever.
16.35: Say: «Save us, God of our salvation, gather us together and deliver us from among the nations, so that we may celebrate your holy name and glory in your praise.»
16.36: Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting. And all the people said, «Amen.» and «Praise the Lord.»
16.37: David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord to continually minister before the ark, according to the task assigned each day.,
16.38: Likewise, Obededom with his brothers, sixty-eight in number, Obededom son of Idithun and Hosah, as gatekeepers,
16.39: Likewise, Zadok the priest and his fellow priests were before the dwelling place of the Lord at the holy place in Gibeon.,
16.40: so that they may continually offer burnt offerings to the Lord, morning and evening, on the altar of burnt offerings, and so that they may fulfill all that is written in the law of the Lord which he imposed on Israel.
16.41: With them were Heman and Idithun and the rest of those who had been chosen and designated by name, to "praise the Lord, for his mercy endures forever."«
16.42: With them, namely, with Heman and Idithun, were trumpets and cymbals for those who were to sound them and instruments for the songs of God. The sons of Idithun were for the gate.
16.43: All the people went home, and David returned to bless his own house.

17.1: When David was settled in his house, David said to Nathan the prophet, «Behold, I am dwelling in a house of cedar, and the ark of the covenant of the Lord is in the tent.»
17.2: Nathan replied to David, "Do what is in your heart, for God is with you."«
17.3: That night, the word of God came to Nathan in these words:
17.4: «Go and tell David, my servant: This is what the Lord says: You are not the one who will build me a house to live in.”.
17.5: For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt until this day, but I have been from tent to tent and from dwelling to dwelling.
17.6: During all the time I traveled with all Israel, did I say a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd my people, saying, “Why do you not build me a house of cedar?”
17.7: Now you shall say to my servant David: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from tending the sheep, to be prince over my people Israel,
17.8: I have been with you everywhere you have gone, I have exterminated all your enemies before you and I have made you a name like the name of the great ones who are on the earth.
17.9: I have assigned a place to my people Israel and I have planted them there, and they will dwell in their home. They will no longer be troubled, nor will the sons of iniquity oppress them anymore, as in the past.
17.10: and as on the day when I appointed judges over my people Israel. I have humbled all your enemies and I announce to you that the Lord will build you a house.
17.11: When your days are fulfilled and you go to be with your ancestors, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your sons, and I will establish his kingdom.
17.12: He is the one who will build me a house, and I will establish his throne forever.
17.13: I will be a father to him and he will be a son to me, and I will not take my grace away from him, as I took it away from him who reigned before you.
17.14: I will establish him forever in my house and in my kingdom, and his throne will be established forever.»
17.15: Nathan spoke to David according to all these words and this entire vision.
17.16: And King David came and stood before the Lord and said, «Who am I, Lord God, and what is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
17.17: And this is still a small thing in your eyes, O God. You have spoken of the house of your servant for a long time and you have regarded me as a man of high rank, O Lord God.
17.18: What more could David tell you about the honor bestowed upon your servant? You know your servant.
17.19: Lord, it is for your servant’s sake and according to your own heart that you have done all these great things, to make all these great things known.
17.20: Lord, there is no one like you and there is no God besides you, according to all we have heard with our ears.
17.21: What other nation is there on earth like your people Israel, whom God came to redeem as his people, to make a name for you through great and wonderful deeds, driving out nations from before your people whom you redeemed from Egypt?
17.22: You have made your people Israel your people forever, and you, Lord, have become their God.
17.23: Now, Lord, let the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house stand forever, and act according to your word.
17.24: May it endure, and may your name be glorified forever, and may it be said: Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, is God to Israel. And may the house of David, your servant, be established before you.
17.25: For you yourself, O my God, have revealed to your servant that you would build him a house, therefore your servant has dared to pray before you.
17.26: Now, Lord, you are God, and you have spoken this pleasing word to your servant.
17.27: Now it has pleased you to bless the house of your servant, so that it may remain forever before you, for what you bless, Lord, is blessed forever.»

18.1: After this, David defeated the Philistines and subdued them, and he delivered Geth and its dependencies out of the hand of the Philistines.
18.2: He defeated the Moabites and the Moabites became slaves to David, bringing him tribute.
18.3: David defeated Hadarezer, king of Zobah, near Hamath, as he was on his way to establish his dominion over the Euphrates River.
18.4: David took from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand horsemen and twenty thousand foot soldiers, David hamstrung all the chariot horses and left only a hundred chariot teams.
18.5: When the Syrians of Damascus came to the aid of Haderazer, king of Zobah, David defeated twenty-two thousand Syrians.
18.6: David placed garrisons in Syria near Damascus; the Syrians were David's slaves, bringing tribute. The Lord gave David victory wherever he went.
18.7: David took the golden shields that were on the servants of Haderazer and brought them to Jerusalem.
18.8: David also took a large quantity of bronze from Thebath and Chun, cities of Haderazer; Solomon made from it the bronze sea, the bronze pillars, and the bronze utensils.
18.9: When Thu, king of Hamath, heard that David had defeated all the forces of Haderazer, king of Zobah,
18.10: He sent Adoram his son to King David to greet him and congratulate him on attacking and defeating Hadarezer, for Thu was constantly at war with Hadarezer. He also sent all kinds of vessels of gold, silver, and bronze.
18.11: King David dedicated them to the Lord with the silver and gold he had taken from all the nations, from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and Amalek.
18.12: Abisai, son of Zervia, defeated the Edomites in the Valley of Salt, numbering eighteen thousand.
18.13: He placed garrisons in Edom, and all Edom became subject to David. And the Lord gave David victory wherever he went.
18.14: David reigned over all Israel and he did justice and righteousness for all his people.
18.15: Joab, son of Zeruiah, was in command of the army; Jehoshaphat, son of Ahilud, was recorder;,
18.16: Zadok, son of Ahitob, and Ahimelech, son of Abiathar, were priests, and Susa was secretary.,
18.17: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, was chief of the Cherethites and the Phelethites, and the sons of David were first at the king's side.

19.1: After that, Naas, king of the sons of Ammon, died and his son reigned in his place.
19.2: David said, «I will show kindness to Hanon son of Naash, because his father has shown me kindness.» And David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. When David’s servants arrived in the land of the Ammonites to comfort Hanon,
19.3: The princes of the Ammonites said to Hanon, «Do you think that David is sending you comforters to honor your father? Is it not to reconnoiter and destroy and explore the land that his servants have come to you?»
19.4: Then Hanon seized David's servants, shaved them, and cut their clothes halfway up to their buttocks, and sent them away.
19.5: David was informed of what had happened to his men, and he sent messengers to meet them, for the men were in great confusion. The king told them, "Stay in Jericho until your beards have grown back, and then you may return."«
19.6: The Ammonites saw that they had made themselves odious to David and Hanon, and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and horsemen from the Syrians of Mesopotamia and from the Syrians of Maah and Zobah.
19.7: They hired thirty-two thousand chariots and the king of Maah with his people, who came and camped near Medaba. The Ammonites gathered from their cities and came to battle.
19.8: David learned of this and sent Joab and the entire army, the valiant warriors, against them.
19.9: The sons of Ammon went out and lined up in battle formation at the city gate, while the kings who had come were apart in the countryside.
19.10: When Joab saw that there was a battle front in front of and behind him, he chose a corps from among all the elite of Israel and positioned it opposite the Syrians.,
19.11: And he placed the rest of the people under the command of his brother Abishai, arranging them opposite the Ammonites. He said:
19.12: «If the Syrians are stronger than me, you will come to my aid, and if the Ammonites are stronger than you, I will help you.”.
19.13: Be steadfast and let us fight valiantly for our people and for the cities of our God, and may the Lord do what seems good in his eyes.»
19.14: So Joab and the people with him advanced against the Syrians to attack them, but they fled before him.
19.15: The Ammonites, seeing that the Syrians had fled, also fled before Abishai, Joab's brother, and returned to the city, and Joab returned to Jerusalem.
19.16: The Syrians, seeing that they had been defeated before Israel, sent messengers to bring over the Syrians who were on the other side of the river, and Sophah, commander of Hadarezer's army, marched in front of them.
19.17: David received the news and, having assembled all Israel, he crossed the Jordan, came to them, and prepared an attack against them. David drew up his army in battle formation against the Syrians. They engaged him in battle.,
19.18: But the Syrians fled before Israel, and David killed the horses of seven thousand chariots and forty thousand foot soldiers of the Syrians; he also put to death Sophah, the commander of the army.
19.19: Hadarezer's vassals, seeing themselves defeated before Israel, made peace with David and became his subjects. And the Syrians refused to come to the aid of the Ammonites any longer.

20.1: At the time of the new year, when kings go to war, Joab led a large army, ravaged the land of the Ammonites, and besieged Rabbah. But David remained in Jerusalem. Joab defeated Rabbah and destroyed it.
20.2: David took the crown off their king's head and found in it a talent of gold, and on it was a precious stone, which was placed on David's head. And he carried off a very great deal of plunder from the city.
20.3: As for the people who were there, he condemned them to forced labor as sawyers, stonecutters, or woodcutters, and David treated all the cities of the Ammonites in the same way. And David returned to Jerusalem with all the people.
20.4: After this, a battle took place at Gazer with the Philistines. Then Saboshai the Husathite killed Shaphia, one of the descendants of Rapha, and the Philistines were humiliated.
20.5: There was another battle with the Philistines, and Elhanan, son of Jair, killed Lahmi, brother of Goliath, of Geth; the shaft of his spear was like a loom roller.
20.6: There was yet another battle at Geth. There was a man of great height, who had six fingers on each hand and on each foot, twenty-four in all, and he too was descended from Rapha.
20.7: He insulted Israel, and Jonathan, son of Shema, David's brother, killed him.
20.8: These men were sons of Rapha, in Geth; they perished by the hand of David and by the hand of his servants.

21.1: Satan stood against Israel and incited David to take a census of Israel.
21.2: And David said to Joab and the leaders of the people, «Go, count Israel from Beersheba to Dan and bring me a report, so that I may know their number.»
21.3: Joab replied, «May the Lord add to his people a hundred times what they are. O king, my lord, are they not all my lord’s slaves? Why then does my lord ask this? Why bring sin upon Israel?»
21.4: But the king's word prevailed against Joab. Joab departed and traveled throughout all Israel and returned to Jerusalem.
21.5: And Joab gave to David the task of counting the people: there were in all Israel eleven hundred thousand men who drew the sword and in Judah four hundred seventy thousand men who drew the sword.
21.6: He did not take a census of Levi and Benjamin among them, because the king's order was repugnant to Joab.
21.7: God disapproved of this matter and struck Israel.
21.8: And David said to God, «I have sinned greatly in doing this. Now please take away the iniquity of your servant, for I have acted very foolishly.»
21.9: The Lord spoke to Gad, David's seer, saying:
21.10: «Go and speak to David, saying: »This is what the Lord says: I have set before you three things; choose one of them, and I will do it for you.’”
21.11: Gad came to David and said to him, «This is what the Lord says: Choose,
21.12: or three years of famine, or three months during which you will be carried away before your adversaries and struck down by the sword of your enemies, or three days during which the sword of the Lord and pestilence will be in the land, and the angel of the Lord will bring destruction throughout all the territory of Israel. Now consider what answer I should give to the one who sent me.»
21.13: David said to Gad, «I am in great distress. Oh, that I might fall into the hands of the Lord, for his mercies are very great, and that I might not fall into the hands of men.»
21.14: And the Lord sent a plague upon Israel, and seventy thousand men in Israel fell.
21.15: And God sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it, and while he was destroying it, the Lord saw and relented concerning this evil, and he said to the angel who was destroying, «Enough! Now withdraw your hand.» The angel of the Lord was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jesubean.
21.16: David looked up and saw the angel of the Lord standing between heaven and earth, with a drawn sword in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders, covered with sackcloth, fell facedown to the ground.
21.17: And David said to God, «Did I not say to take a census of the people? I am the one who has sinned and done wrong, but these, these sheep, what have they done? Lord my God, let your hand be upon me and upon my father’s house, but not upon your people to destroy them.»
21.18: The angel of the Lord commanded Gad to tell David to go up and erect an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
21.19: David went up, according to the word of Gad which he had spoken in the name of the Lord.
21.20: Ornan, having turned around, saw the angel and he hid himself, he and his four sons: Ornan was threshing wheat at that time.
21.21: When David arrived near Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David, immediately coming out of the threshing floor, he bowed down before David, his face to the ground.
21.22: David said to Ornan, «Give me the site of the threshing floor so I can build an altar to the Lord there. Give it to me for its full value in silver, so that the plague may be averted from the people.»
21.23: Ornan said to David, «Take it and let my lord the king do as he pleases. Look, I will give the oxen for the burnt offering, the sledges for the wood, and the wheat for the grain offering; I will give all of this.»
21.24: But King David said to Ornan, «No. I will buy it for its full value in silver, for I will not take what belongs to the Lord, nor will I offer a burnt offering that costs me nothing.»
21.25: David gave Ornan a weight of six hundred shekels of gold for the site.
21.26: And David built an altar there to the Lord and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. He cried out to the Lord, and the Lord answered him by fire that came down from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.
21.27: And the Lord spoke to the angel, and the angel put his sword back into its sheath.
21.28: At that time, David, seeing that the Lord had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, offered sacrifices there.
21.29: The tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses had built in the desert, and the altar of burnt offerings were at that time on the holy place at Gibeon.
21.30: But David could not go before that altar to honor God, because he had been terrified by the sword of the angel of the Lord.

22.1: And David said, «This is the house of the Lord God, and this is the altar for the burnt offerings of Israel.»
22.2: David ordered that the foreigners who were in the land of Israel be gathered together, and he appointed stonemasons to cut dressed stones for the construction of the house of God.
22.3: David also prepared a large quantity of iron for the nails on the door panels and for the hooks, as well as an incalculable quantity of bronze.,
22.4: and countless cedar logs, for the Sidonians and Tyrians had brought David an abundance of cedar logs.
22.5: David said, «My son Solomon is young and weak, and the house that will be built for the Lord must be exceedingly magnificent, so that it may be renowned and glorious throughout all the lands; therefore I want to make preparations for it.» And David made many preparations before his death.
22.6: David called Solomon his son and ordered him to build a house for the Lord, the God of Israel.
22.7: David said to Solomon, «My son, I had it in my heart to build a house in the name of the Lord my God.
22.8: But the word of the Lord came to me, saying: You have shed much blood and have waged great wars; you shall not build a house for my name, because you have shed much blood on the earth before me.
22.9: Behold, a son shall be born unto you, who shall be a quiet man; I will give him rest from all his enemies around him, for Solomon shall be his name, and I will give peace and quiet in Israel in his days.
22.10: He will build a house for my name; he will be my son and I will be his father, and I will establish his royal throne over Israel forever.
22.11: Now, my son, may the Lord be with you, so that you may prosper and build the house of the Lord your God, according to what he has said about you.
22.12: Only grant the Lord wisdom and understanding when he gives you authority over Israel, so that you may keep the law of the Lord your God.
22.13: Then you will prosper if you carefully observe the statutes and ordinances that the Lord commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and courageous; do not be afraid or discouraged.
22.14: Behold, by my efforts, I have prepared for the house of the Lord one hundred thousand talents of gold, one million talents of silver, as for the bronze and iron, they cannot be weighed, for there is a quantity of them, I have also prepared wood and stones and you shall add more.
22.15: You have with you a multitude of workers, stonecutters, craftsmen of stone and wood, and men skilled in every kind of work.
22.16: Gold, silver, bronze, and iron are countless. Arise. Get to work, and may the Lord be with you.»
22.17: David commanded all the leaders of Israel to come to the aid of Solomon his son, saying:
22.18: «Is not the Lord your God with you? Has he not given you rest on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hands, and the land is subdued before the Lord and before his people.”.
22.19: Now, set your heart and soul to seek the Lord your God, and arise and build the sanctuary of the Lord God, so that you may bring the ark of the covenant of the Lord and the holy vessels of God into the house that will be built in the name of the Lord.»

23.1: David, old and full of years, established Solomon his son as king over Israel.
23.2: He gathered all the leaders of Israel, the priests and the Levites.
23.3: The Levites were counted, from the age of thirty years and above; their number, per head and per man, was thirty-eight thousand.
23.4: And David said, «Of these, let twenty-four thousand be appointed to the offices of the house of the Lord, let six thousand be scribes and magistrates,
23.5: four thousand gatekeepers, and four thousand praising the Lord with the instruments I made for praising him.»
23.6: David divided them into classes, according to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
23.8: The sons of Leedan: the chief Jahiel, Zetham and Joel: three.
23.9: The sons of Shemei: Salomith, Hoziel and Aran: three. These are the heads of families descended from Leedan.
23.10: The sons of Shemei: Leheth, Ziza, Jaus, and Bariah. These are the four sons of Shemei,
23.11: Leheth was the chief and Ziza the second, Jaus and Bariah did not have many sons and they were counted, according to their family, in one class.
23.12: Sons of Caath: Amram, Isaar, Hebron and Oziel: four.
23.13: Sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to be sanctified as most holy, he and his sons forever, to burn incense before the Lord, to minister to him, and to bless in his name forever.
23.14: As for Moses, the man of God, his sons were counted in the tribe of Levi.
23.15: Sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
23.17: The sons of Eliezer were: Rehobiah, the chief; Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehobiah were very numerous.
23.18: Son of Isaar: Salomith, the leader.
23.19: The sons of Hebron: Jeriau the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jecmaam the fourth,
23.20: Sons of Oziel: Micha, the chief, Jesiah, the second.
23.21: Sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi. Sons of Moholi: Eleazar and Cis.
23.22: Eleazar died without having any sons, but only daughters; the sons of Cish, their brothers, took them as wives.
23.23: Sons of Musi: Molech, Eder and Jerome: three.
23.24: These were the sons of Levi according to their families, the heads of families according to how they were numbered, counted by name by head, they did the work of the service of the house of the Lord, from the age of twenty years and above.
23.25: For David said, «The Lord, the God of Israel, has given rest to his people, and he will dwell forever in Jerusalem.”
23.26: And as for the Levites, they will no longer have to carry the Tabernacle and all the utensils for its service.»
23.27: It was according to David's last orders that the census of the sons of Levi from the age of twenty years and above was taken.
23.28: For their function was to be at the disposal of Aaron's sons for the service of the house of the Lord, concerning the courts and the chambers, the purification of all the holy things, and the work of service in the house of God,
23.29: concerning the breads of the proposition, the flour for the oblations, the unleavened cakes, what is cooked on the pan and what is mixed, everything that had to be weighed and measured.
23.30: They were required to present themselves every morning and every evening, in order to celebrate and praise the Lord.,
23.31: and to offer all burnt offerings to the Lord on the Sabbaths, the new moons, and the feasts, according to the number that the law prescribes to be offered perpetually before the Lord.
23.32: They were to devote themselves to the care of the tent of meeting, to the care of the holy things, and to the care of Aaron's sons, their brothers, for the service of the house of the Lord.

24.1: As for the sons of Aaron, these are their divisions: Sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abiu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
24.2: Nadab and Abiu died before their father, without having any sons, and Eleazar and Ithamar fulfilled the functions of the priesthood.
