24.3: David, Zadok, of the descendants of Eleazar, and Ahimelech, of the descendants of Ithamar, divided Aaron's sons into classes according to their service.
24.4: Among the sons of Eleazar there were more leaders than among the sons of Ithamar, and they were divided as follows: for the sons of Eleazar, sixteen heads of families, and for the sons of Ithamar, eight heads of families.
24.5: They were divided by lot, one after the other, for there were princes of the sanctuary and princes of God among both the sons of Eleazar and the sons of Ithamar.
24.6: Semeiah, son of Nathanael, the secretary, one of the Levites, registered them before the king and the princes, before Zadok the high priest and Ahimelech, son of Abiathar and before the heads of the priestly and Levitical families, one family being chosen by lot for Eleazar, then one family for Ithamar.
24.7: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedei.,
24.8: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
24.9: the fifth in Melchia, the sixth in Maïman,
24.10: the seventh in Accos, the eighth in Abia,
24.11: the ninth to Jesus, the tenth to Shecheniah,
24.12: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jacim,
24.13: the thirteenth to Hoppha, the fourteenth to Isbaab,
24.14: the fifteenth to Belga, the sixteenth to Emmer,
24.15: the seventeenth at Hezir, the eighteenth at Aphses,
24.16: the nineteenth in Pheteiah, the twentieth in Hezekiel,
24.17: the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
24.18: the twenty-third to Dalaiau, the twenty-fourth to Mazziaau.
24.19: These were their divisions according to their service, so that they might come to the house of the Lord, according to the regulations they had received through Aaron their father, as the Lord, the God of Israel, had commanded him.
24.20: These are the leaders of the remaining Levites: of the sons of Amram: Subael, of the sons of Subael: Jehediah,
24.21: of Rohobiah, of the sons of Rohobiah: the chief Jesiah.
24.22: Of the Isaarites: Salemoth, of the sons of Salemoth: Jahath.
24.23: Sons of Hebron: Jeriau the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jecmaan the fourth.
24.24: Sons of Oziel: Micha, of the sons of Micha: Samir,
24.25: Micah's brother: Jesiah, Jesiah's son: Zechariah.
24.26: Sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi.
24.27: Son of Merari, by Uzziau his son: Saam, Zechar and Hebri.
24.28: From Moholi: Eleazar, who had no sons,
24.29: of Cish, the sons of Cish, Jerahmeel.
24.30: Musi's sons: Moholi, Eder and Jerimoth.
24.31: These were the sons of Levi, according to their families. They too, like their brothers, the sons of Aaron, cast lots before King David, before Zadok and Ahimelech, and before the heads of the priestly and Levitical families, the older ones being on the same footing as the younger ones.

25.1: David and the commanders of the army set apart for service some of the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Idithun, who prophesied with the harp, lyre, and cymbals. This is the number of those who were engaged in service:
25.2: The sons of Asaph: Zechariah, Joseph, Nathana, and Asarela, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied according to the king's orders.
25.3: From Idithun, the sons of Idithun: Godolias, Sori, Jeseias, Hasabias, Mathathias and Semei, six, according to the orders of their father Idithun, who prophesied with the lyre to celebrate and praise the Lord.
25.4: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bocciaü, Mathaniau, Oziel, Subuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliatha, Geddelthi, Romemthiezer, Jeshbacassa, Mellothi, Othir and Mahazioth,
25.5: They were all sons of Heman, who saw the king in the words of God, to exalt his power: God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
25.6: All these Levites were under the command of their fathers for the singing of the house of the Lord, having cymbals, lyres and harps for the service of the house of God, under the command of David, Asaph, Idithun and Heman.
25.7: Their number, including their brothers trained in singing to the Lord, all those who had skill, was two hundred and eighty-eight.
25.8: They drew lots for their roles, both small and great, masters and disciples.
25.9: The first lot fell to Joseph for Asaph. The second to Gedaliah: him, his brothers and his sons: twelve.
25.10: The third, to Zechariah: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.11: The fourth, to Isari: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.12: The fifth to Nathania: him, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.13: The sixth to Bocciaü: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.14: The seventh in Israel: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.15: The eighth to Jesaiah: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.16: The ninth to Mathanias: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.17: The tenth to Semeiah: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.18: The eleventh to Azareel: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.19: The twelfth to Hasabias: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.20: The thirteenth at Subuel: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.21: The fourteenth to Mathathias: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.22: The fifteenth to Jerimoth: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.23: The sixteenth to Hananiah: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.24: The seventeenth at Jesbacassa: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.25: The eighteenth to Hanani: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve,
25.26: The nineteenth to Mellothi: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.27: The twentieth to Eliatha: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.28: The twenty-first to Othir: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.29: The twenty-second at Geddelthi: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.30: The twenty-third to Mahazioth: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.
25.31: The twenty-fourth to Romemthiezer: he, his sons and his brothers: twelve.

26.1: These are the classes of the gatekeepers: Of the Korahites: Meselemiah, son of Korah, of the sons of Asaph.
26.2: The sons of Meselemiah: Zacharias the firstborn, Jadihel the second, Zabadias the third, Jathanael the fourth,
26.3: Elam the fifth, Johanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.
26.4: The sons of Obededom: Shemeiah, the firstborn, Jozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nathanael the fifth,
26.5: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Phollathi the eighth, for God had blessed Obededom.
26.6: To his son Semeiah were born sons who were leaders in their father's house, for they were men of great worth.
26.7: Sons of Semeiah: Othni, Raphael, Obed, Elzabad and his brothers, worthy men, Eliu and Samachias.
26.8: All these were sons of Obededom, they, their sons and their brothers, brave and strong men for service, were sixty-two in number, descended from Obededom.
26.9: Meselemia had sons and brothers, all worthy people, numbering eighteen.
26.10: Hosa, one of the sons of Merari, had a son: Semri, the chief. He was not the firstborn, but his father had appointed him chief.,
26.11: Helcias the second, Tabelias the third, Zacharias the fourth. All the sons and brothers of Hosah were thirteen in number.
26.12: These classes of gatekeepers, the leaders of these men, had, alongside their brothers, functions for the service of the house of the Lord.
26.13: They drew lots for each door, small and large, according to their families.
26.14: The lot for the eastern side fell to Selemiah. Lots were cast for his son Zechariah, who was a wise advisor, and the northern side fell to him.
26.15: The southern side fell to Obededom and the storehouse to his sons.
26.16: Sephim and Hosah were assigned the western side, near the Shalleketh Gate, on the way up: one guard was opposite the other.
26.17: There were six Levites in the east, four per day in the north, four per day in the south, and four in the stores, two by two.,
26.18: on the side of the dependencies, to the west, four towards the road, two at the dependencies.
26.19: These are the classes of gatekeepers, from among the sons of the Korahites and the sons of Merari.
26.20: The Levites, their brothers, had the stewardship of the treasures of the house of God and the treasures of the consecrated things.
26.21: Among the sons of Ledan, namely the sons of the Gershonites descended from Ledan, heads of the families of Ledan the Gershonite, was Jehiel,
26.22: Jehiel's sons, Zathan and Joel, his brother, were in charge of the treasures of the house of the Lord.
26.23: Among the Amramites, the Isaarites, the Hebronites, and the Uzihelites,
26.24: It was Subuel, son of Gershom, son of Moses, who was the chief steward of the treasury.
26.25: Among his brothers descended from Eliezer, whose son was Rahabiah, whose son was Jesse, whose son was Joram, whose son was Zechariah, whose son was Shelemith,
26.26: It was Selemith and his brothers who were in charge of all the treasures of the holy things that King David had consecrated, the heads of families, with the commanders of thousands and hundreds, and the commanders of the army:
26.27: They had dedicated them from the spoils taken in war, for the upkeep of the house of the Lord.
26.28: Everything that Samuel the seer, Saul son of Cish, Abner son of Ner, Joab son of Zervia, or anyone else had dedicated was in the hand of Shelemith and his brothers.
26.29: Among the Isaarites, Chonenias and his sons were employed in foreign affairs in Israel, as scribes and as magistrates.
26.30: Among the Hebronites, Hasabiah and his brothers, men of great worth, numbering one thousand seven hundred, had the supervision of Israel on the other side of the Jordan, to the west, for all the affairs of the Lord and the service of the king.
26.31: For the Hebronites, Jeriah was the leader. For the Hebronites, according to their genealogies, by families, a search was made in the fortieth year of David's reign, and among them were found men of great worth at Jazer-gilead.,
26.32: And his brothers, worthy men, numbered two thousand seven hundred heads of families. King David appointed them over the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh for all matters of God and the king's business.

27.1: The children of Israel, according to their number, heads of families, commanders of thousands and hundreds, officers in the king's service for all matters concerning the divisions, those arriving and those departing, month by month, throughout all the months of the year, each division being twenty-four thousand men, were:
27.2: At the head of the first division, for the first month, was Jeshboam son of Zabdiel and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.3: He was one of the sons of Peres and he commanded all the leaders of the troops in the first month.
27.4: At the head of the division of the second month was Dudia the Ahohite, Macelloth the prince, was a leader of his division and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.5: The leader of the third division, for the third month, was Banaias son of the priest Jehoiada chief and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.6: This Banaias was the hero of the Thirty, at the head of the Thirty, Amizabad his son, was a leader of his division.
27.7: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and after him, Zabadias his son, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.8: The fifth, for the fifth month, was the chief Samaoth the Izrahite, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.9: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Hira son of Acces of Tekua, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.10: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helles the Phallonite of the sons of Ephraim, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.11: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Soboshai the Husathite of the Zarahite family, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.12: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer of Anathoth of the sons of Benjamin, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.13: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Marai of Netophah of the family of the Zaharites and there were in his division twenty-four thousand men.
27.14: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Banaiah of Pharathon of the sons of Ephraim, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.15: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Holdai of Netophah of the family of Othniel, and there were twenty-four thousand men in his division.
27.16: And there was at the head of the tribes of Israel: of the Reubenites: Eliezer son of Zechariah. Of the Simeonites: Shaphatias son of Maah.
27.17: Of the Levites: Hasabiah son of Camuel. Of the family of Aaron: Zadok,
27.18: of Judah: Eli, brother of David of Issachar, Amri, son of Michael.
27.19: From Zebulun: Jeshmaiah son of Obadiah. From Naphtali: Jerimoth son of Ozriel.
27.20: Of the sons of Ephraim: Hoshea son of Uzaziu. Of the half-tribe of Manasseh: Joel son of Phaedaiah.
27.21: From the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead: Jaddo son of Zechariah. From Benjamin: Jasiel son of Abner.
27.22: From Dan: Ezrihel son of Jeroham. These are the princes of the tribes of Israel.
27.23: David did not take a census of those who were twenty years old and under, because the Lord had said that he would multiply Israel like the stars of heaven.
27.24: Joab son of Zervia had begun the census, but he did not finish it, the anger of the Lord having come upon Israel because of this, and the result was not recorded in the rolls of the chronicles of King David.
27.25: Asmoth son of Adiel was in charge of the king's treasures. Jonathan son of Uzziah was in charge of the treasures in the fields, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers.
27.26: Ezri son of Chelub, on the farm laborers who cultivated the land.
27.27: Semei of Ramah, on the vineyards. Zabdiah of Sapham, on the wine stores in the vineyards.
27.28: Balanan of Geder, concerning the olive and sycamore trees in the Sephelah. Joash, concerning the stores of oil.
27.29: Sethrai of Sharon, over the cattle that grazed in Sharon. Shaphat son of Adli, over the cattle in the valleys.
27.30: Ubil the Ishmaelite, on camels. Jadias of Meronath, on donkeys.
27.31: Jaziz the Hagarenite, in charge of the sheep. All these were stewards of King David's property.
27.32: Jonathan, David's uncle, was an advisor; he was a man of understanding and knowledge. Jahiel son of Hashamoni was with the king's sons.
27.33: Achitophel was an advisor to the king, and Chusai the Archean was a friend of the king.,
27.34: Jehoiada son of Benaiah and Abiathar succeeded Ahithophel. Joab was commander of the king's army.

28.1: David gathered together in Jerusalem all the princes of Israel: the princes of the tribes, the commanders of the divisions that served the king, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, the overseers of all the possessions and livestock of the king and his sons, as well as the overseers and the mighty men, all the mighty soldiers.
28.2: King David stood up and said, «Listen to me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord and for the footstool of our God, and I was making preparations for the construction.
28.3: But God said to me: You shall not build a house for my name, because you are a man of war and have shed blood.
28.4: The Lord, the God of Israel, has chosen me from all my father’s house to be king over Israel forever. For he has chosen Judah to be leader, and from the house of Judah he has chosen my father’s house, and from among my father’s sons he has been pleased with me to make me king over all Israel.
28.5: And among all my sons, for the Lord has given me many sons, he has chosen my son Solomon to sit on the throne of the kingship of the Lord over Israel.
28.6: He said to me: It is Solomon, your son, who will build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him for my son and I will be a father to him.
28.7: I will establish his kingship forever, if he remains committed, as he is today, to the practice of my commandments and my ordinances.
28.8: Now, in the sight of all Israel, of the assembly of the Lord, and before our God who hears us, keep and carefully observe all the commandments of the Lord your God, so that you may possess this good land and pass it on as an inheritance to your children after you forever.
28.9: And you, Solomon, my son, know the God of your father and serve him with a perfect heart and a devoted soul, for the Lord searches all hearts and understands every plan of the thoughts. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will reject you forever.
28.10: See now that the Lord has chosen you to build a house that will be his sanctuary. Be strong and get to work!»
28.11: David gave to Solomon his son the design of the portico and the buildings, treasury chambers, upper chambers, inner chambers attached to it, as well as the mercy seat,
28.12: the plan of everything he had in mind concerning the courts of the house of the Lord and all the surrounding chambers, for the treasures of the house of God and the treasures of the sanctuary,
28.13: for the classes of priests and Levites, for all the work of the service of the house of the Lord, and for all the utensils used in the service of the house of the Lord,
28.14: the model of the gold utensils, with the weight of the gold, for all the utensils of each service, the model of all the silver utensils, with the weight, for all the utensils of each service,
28.15: the weight of the gold candlesticks and their gold lamps, according to the weight of each candlestick and its lamps, and the weight of the silver candlesticks, according to the weight of each candlestick,
28.16: the weight of gold for the tables of the showbread, for each table, and the weight of silver for the silver tables,
28.17: the model of forks, ewers and cups of pure gold, of gold amphorae, with the weight of each amphora, of silver amphorae, with the weight of each amphora,
28.18: from the altar of incense made of refined gold, according to its weight, the pattern of the chariot of God, golden cherubim spreading their wings and covering the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
28.19: «All this,» said David, “all the works of this pattern, the Lord instructed me in a writing that came to me from his own hand.”
28.20: David said to Solomon his son, «Be strong and courageous. Get to work. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the Lord God, my God, will be with you; he will not leave you nor forsake you until all the work for the service of the house of the Lord is finished.”.
28.21: And behold, with you are the divisions of priests and Levites for all the service of the house of God; with you, for every kind of work, are also all the devoted and skilled men, for all the work; finally, the leaders and the people for the execution of all your commands.»

29.1: King David said to the whole assembly: «My son Solomon, the only one whom God has chosen, is young and weak, and the work is considerable, for this palace is not for a man, but for the Lord God.
29.2: I have put all my effort into preparing for the house of my God gold for articles of gold, silver for articles of silver, bronze for articles of bronze, iron for articles of iron, wood for articles of wood, onyx stones and setting stones, ornamental stones and stones of various colors, all kinds of precious stones and marble stones in abundance.
29.3: Moreover, in my affection for the house of God, the gold and silver that I possess of my own, I give to the house of my God, besides all that I have prepared for the house of the sanctuary:
29.4: three thousand talents of gold, gold from Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the chambers,
29.5: Gold for gold objects, silver for silver objects and for all the work of craftsmen. Who today wants to spontaneously fill their hand with offerings for the Lord?»
29.6: The heads of families, the princes of the tribes of Israel, the leaders of thousands and hundreds, as well as the king's stewards, voluntarily made offerings.
29.7: They gave for the work of the house of God five thousand talents of gold, ten thousand darics, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze and one hundred thousand talents of iron.
29.8: Those who possessed precious stones gave them, for the treasury of the house of the Lord, into the hands of Jahiel the Gershonite.
29.9: The people rejoiced at their freewill offerings, for it was with a perfect heart that they made these freewill offerings to the Lord; King David also had great joy.
29.10: David blessed the Lord in the sight of all the assembly and David said: "Blessed are you, from everlasting to everlasting, Lord, God of our father Israel.
29.11: To you, Lord, belong the greatness, the power, the magnificence, the splendor, and the glory, for all in heaven and on earth belongs to you; to you, Lord, belongs the kingship; to you shall rise supremely above all.
29.12: From you come wealth and glory, you rule over all, in your hand is strength and power, and to your hand it belongs to give greatness and solidity to all things.
29.13: Therefore, O our God, we praise you and celebrate your glorious name.
29.14: For who am I and who are my people, that we should have the power and the strength to make such offerings willingly? Everything comes from you and we give you what we have received from your hand.
29.15: For we are strangers and colonists before you, as were all our fathers; our days on earth are like a shadow, and there is no hope.
29.16: Lord, our God, all these riches that we have prepared to build you a house for your holy name, they come from your hand and everything belongs to you.
29.17: I know, O my God, that you search hearts and that you love righteousness, so I have willingly made all these offerings in the righteousness of my heart and I now see with joy your people who are here willingly making their offerings to you.
29.18: Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac and Israel, our fathers, keep this disposition forever as the form of the feelings of the heart of your people and keep their hearts turned towards you.
29.19: And to my son Solomon, give a perfect heart to observe your commandments, your precepts, and your laws, to put them all into practice, and to build the palace for which I have made preparations.»
29.20: David said to the whole assembly, «Therefore bless the Lord your God.» And all the assembly blessed the Lord, the God of their fathers; they bowed down and prostrated themselves before the Lord and before the king.
29.21: The day after that day, they sacrificed victims to the Lord and offered as burnt offerings to the Lord a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their customary drink offerings and other sacrifices in great numbers for all Israel,
29.22: They ate and drank that day before the Lord with great joy. For the second time, they proclaimed Solomon, son of David, king, and anointed him as ruler before the Lord, and they anointed Zadok as high priest.
29.23: Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king, in place of David, his father; he prospered and all Israel obeyed him.
29.24: All the leaders and valiant men, even all the sons of King David, submitted to King Solomon.
29.25: The Lord raised Solomon to the highest degree in the sight of all Israel, and gave his reign a glory that no king in Israel had ever had before him.
29.26: David, son of Jesse, reigned over all Israel.
29.27: The time he reigned over Israel was forty years, he reigned seven years in Hebron and he reigned thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
29.28: He died in a happy old age, full of days, riches and glory, and Solomon his son reigned in his place.
29.29: The acts of King David, from first to last, are written in the history of Samuel the seer, in the history of Nathan the prophet, and in the history of Gad the seer.,
29.30: with all his reign and all his exploits and the vicissitudes that befell him, as well as Israel and all the kingdoms of other countries.
